# Feature: CLI Runtime Scan

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-08 -->

## Intent
Wire the existing `@autoguide/runtime` DOM scanner into `autoguide scan` so runtime facts with `runtime_dom` provenance are captured via Playwright (no manual Inspector), merged into the knowledge graph, and persisted to `.autoguide/`.

## Preconditions
- `autoguide.config.json` exists (`autoguide init`)
- For `--runtime`: app reachable at `baseUrl` (config or `--base-url` / `--runtime-url`)
- Playwright optional — runtime capture skips gracefully when unavailable

## Happy Path
- [ ] `autoguide scan --runtime` captures DOM snapshots at configured `baseUrl`
- [ ] Runtime facts use `runtime_dom` provenance via `runtimeSnapshotToFacts`
- [ ] Facts appear in `.autoguide/facts.json` after merge
- [ ] `runtime-snapshot.json` written when runtime scan runs
- [ ] Routes scanned: source routes, else Playwright visited routes, else `/`

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Without `--runtime`, scan behavior unchanged (no runtime facts)
- [ ] Playwright missing → warning, scan continues with source/playwright import only
- [ ] `safeMode` skips destructive route labels (same as crawl)
- [ ] `--runtime-url` overrides base URL for runtime capture only

## Regression
- [ ] `integrations/hr-workflows` scan test still passes without `--runtime`
- [ ] Existing scanner/playwright/cli tests pass

## Assumptions
- Runtime capture uses Playwright headless + in-page scan (browser context); Node does not call `scanDom` directly
- Local dev: dev server at `baseUrl`; CI: start app or skip `--runtime`

## Implementation Notes
- `packages/playwright/src/browser-scan-dom.ts` — self-contained `browserScanDom` for `page.evaluate`
- `packages/playwright/src/capture-runtime.ts` — `captureRuntimeSnapshots`, `mergeRuntimeSnapshots`
- `packages/cli/src/commands/scan.ts` — `--runtime` / `--runtime-url`, merge via `mergeScanResults`, `runtime-snapshot.json`
- `packages/config` — `scan.runtime` opt-in default
- Tests: `merge-facts.test.ts`, `capture-runtime.test.ts`, `runtime-scan.integration.test.ts`
